requirements.description: Every fifth-wave child bead maps to a pushed commit, source paths, test or fixture paths, docs or evidence paths, validation commands, close reason, and claim-boundary text.

The checklist covers provider streaming backpressure, tool artifact lifecycle, branch-heavy replay minimization, TUI degradation, extension permission drift, validation refresh scheduling, source boundaries, pushed refs, and quality gates.
A passing gate may close the final gate and parent epic only after the closeout commit lands and the branch is pushed. A failing gate must emit follow-up beads instead of optimistic closeout.
The closeout gate is evidence over Beads, git, source files, tests, RCH, Agent Mail posture, UBS, and child artifacts. It does not replace those sources of truth.
The closeout gate must not mutate coordination state, regenerate evidence, enforce runtime throttles, or make release-facing performance, capacity, benchmark, or strict drop-in claims.
